video_frame(3) Library Functions Manual video_frame(3)

NAME

video_frame - Video Frames


- 📹 High-performance video frame management with double-buffering and lock-free operations

SYNOPSIS

Files


file video_frame.c
🎬 Video frame buffer management for client-specific ASCII rendering

Data Structures


struct video_frame_t
Video frame structure. struct video_frame_buffer_t
Video frame buffer manager. struct video_frame_stats_t
Frame statistics structure. struct simple_frame_swap_t
Simple atomic frame swap structure.

Macros


#define VIDEO_FRAME_BUFFER_SIZE MAX_FRAME_BUFFER_SIZE
Video frame buffer size (2MB per frame). #define MAX_FRAME_BUFFERS 2
Maximum number of frame buffers (double buffering).

Functions


video_frame_buffer_t * video_frame_buffer_create (const char *client_id)
Create a double-buffered video frame manager. void video_frame_buffer_destroy (video_frame_buffer_t *vfb)
Destroy frame buffer and free all resources. video_frame_t * video_frame_begin_write (video_frame_buffer_t *vfb)
Writer API: Start writing a new frame. void video_frame_commit (video_frame_buffer_t *vfb)
Writer API: Commit the frame and swap buffers. const video_frame_t * video_frame_get_latest (video_frame_buffer_t *vfb)
Reader API: Get latest frame if available. void video_frame_get_stats (video_frame_buffer_t *vfb, video_frame_stats_t *stats)
Get frame statistics for quality monitoring. simple_frame_swap_t * simple_frame_swap_create (void)
Create a simple atomic frame swap. void simple_frame_swap_destroy (simple_frame_swap_t *sfs)
Destroy simple frame swap and free resources. void simple_frame_swap_update (simple_frame_swap_t *sfs, const void *data, size_t size)
Update frame data in simple frame swap. const video_frame_t * simple_frame_swap_get (simple_frame_swap_t *sfs)
Get current frame from simple frame swap.

Detailed Description

📹 High-performance video frame management with double-buffering and lock-free operations

This header provides a high-performance video frame management system inspired by WebRTC, Zoom, and Google Meet architectures. It implements a lock-free double-buffering system with atomic pointer swaps for zero-copy frame transfer.

CORE FEATURES:

  • Zero-copy frame transfer (pointer swaps only)
  • Lock-free read operations (reader never blocks writer)
  • Latest-frame-wins semantics (old frames are dropped, not queued)
  • Double buffering with atomic pointer swaps
  • Comprehensive quality metrics tracking
  • Pre-allocated buffers for zero-allocation operation
  • Frame sequence numbering for drop detection

ARCHITECTURE:

The system uses a double-buffering pattern:

  • Front Buffer: Currently being displayed (reader owns)
  • Back Buffer: Currently being written (writer owns)
  • Atomic swap: Makes new frame available without blocking

WRITER API:

1.
Call video_frame_begin_write() to get back buffer pointer
2.
Write frame data directly to back buffer
3.
Call video_frame_commit() to atomically swap buffers

READER API:

1.
Call video_frame_get_latest() to get current frame
2.
Returns NULL if no new frame since last read
3.
Lock-free operation never blocks writer

PERFORMANCE BENEFITS:

  • Zero-copy eliminates memcpy overhead
  • Lock-free reads minimize latency
  • Pre-allocated buffers avoid runtime allocations
  • Latest-frame-wins prevents buffer buildup
  • Metrics enable adaptive quality control

QUALITY METRICS:

The system tracks:

  • Total frames received
  • Total frames dropped
  • Frame drop rate
  • Average decode time
  • Average render time

These metrics enable adaptive streaming quality based on performance.

Note

Frame data is not owned by the frame structure - it points to pre-allocated buffers managed by the frame buffer.

The writer must call video_frame_commit() after writing each frame to make it available to readers.

Readers should check for NULL returns from video_frame_get_latest() to handle cases where no new frame is available.

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

October 2025

Macro Definition Documentation

#define MAX_FRAME_BUFFERS 2

#include <include/ascii-chat/video/rgba/video_frame.h>

Maximum number of frame buffers (double buffering).

Definition at line 95 of file video_frame.h.

#define VIDEO_FRAME_BUFFER_SIZE MAX_FRAME_BUFFER_SIZE

#include <include/ascii-chat/video/rgba/video_frame.h>

Video frame buffer size (2MB per frame).

Definition at line 93 of file video_frame.h.

Function Documentation

simple_frame_swap_t * simple_frame_swap_create (void )

#include <include/ascii-chat/video/rgba/video_frame.h>

Create a simple atomic frame swap.

Returns

Pointer to newly created frame swap, or NULL on failure

Creates a lightweight frame swap structure for basic use cases that don't need statistics tracking. Uses atomic pointer swaps for lock-free operation.

Note

This is a simpler alternative to video_frame_buffer_t.

Pre-allocates two frame structures that alternate on each update.

No statistics tracking (lighter weight than full frame buffer).

Warning

Must call simple_frame_swap_destroy() to free resources.

Definition at line 305 of file video_frame.c.

305                                                     {
306   simple_frame_swap_t *sfs = SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(simple_frame_swap_t), simple_frame_swap_t *);
307 
308   // Pre-allocate both frames
309   const size_t frame_size = (size_t)MAX_FRAME_BUFFER_SIZE;
310   sfs->frame_a.data = SAFE_MALLOC(frame_size, void *);
311   sfs->frame_b.data = SAFE_MALLOC(frame_size, void *);
312 
313   atomic_store_u64(&sfs->current_frame, (uintptr_t)&sfs->frame_a);
314   atomic_store_bool(&sfs->use_frame_a, false); // Next write goes to frame_b
315 
316   return sfs;
317 }




References atomic_store_bool(), atomic_store_u64(),
    simple_frame_swap_t::current_frame, video_frame_t::data,
    simple_frame_swap_t::frame_a, simple_frame_swap_t::frame_b,
    MAX_FRAME_BUFFER_SIZE, SAFE_CALLOC, SAFE_MALLOC, and
    simple_frame_swap_t::use_frame_a.






void
  simple_frame_swap_destroy (simple_frame_swap_t * sfs)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/rgba/video_frame.h>


Destroy simple frame swap and free resources.


Parameters


sfs Frame swap to destroy (can be NULL)


Destroys the simple frame swap and frees all associated resources.
    Safe to call multiple times or with NULL pointer.


Definition at line 319 of file video_frame.c.


319                                                          {
320   if (!sfs) {
321     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Simple frame swap is NULL");
322     return;
323   }
324   SAFE_FREE(sfs->frame_a.data);
325   SAFE_FREE(sfs->frame_b.data);
326   SAFE_FREE(sfs);
327 }






References video_frame_t::data, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    simple_frame_swap_t::frame_a, simple_frame_swap_t::frame_b,
    SAFE_FREE, and SET_ERRNO.






const video_frame_t * simple_frame_swap_get
  (simple_frame_swap_t * sfs)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/rgba/video_frame.h>


Get current frame from simple frame swap.


Parameters


sfs Frame swap (must not be NULL)


Returns


Pointer to current frame, or NULL if no frame
  available


Gets a pointer to the current frame. This is a lock-free read
    operation that reads the atomic frame pointer. Returns NULL if no frame has
    been updated yet.




Note


Frame pointer is valid until next
  simple_frame_swap_update() call.



Lock-free operation (uses atomic pointer read).




Definition at line 353 of file video_frame.c.


353                                                                      {
354   if (!sfs) {
355     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Simple frame swap is NULL");
356     return NULL;
357   }
358   uintptr_t frame_ptr = atomic_load_u64(&sfs->current_frame);
359   return (const video_frame_t *)(void *)frame_ptr; // NOLINT(bugprone-casting-through-void,performance-no-int-to-ptr)
360 }






References atomic_load_u64(),
    simple_frame_swap_t::current_frame, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, and
    SET_ERRNO.






void
  simple_frame_swap_update (simple_frame_swap_t * sfs, const void * data,
  size_t size)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/rgba/video_frame.h>


Update frame data in simple frame swap.


Parameters


sfs Frame swap (must not be NULL)


data Frame data pointer (must not be NULL)


size Size of frame data in bytes


Updates the current frame with new data. Atomically swaps the
    active frame pointer to make new data available to readers. This is a
    zero-copy operation (only pointer is updated, data is not copied).




Note


Frame data should remain valid until next update or
  destruction.



Atomic pointer swap ensures readers see consistent frame
  pointer.




Alternates between frame_a and frame_b on each update.




Warning


Frame data pointer must remain valid until next
  update.


Definition at line 329 of file video_frame.c.


329                                                                                        {
330   if (!sfs || !data) {
331     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Simple frame swap or data is NULL");
332     return;
333   }
334 
335   // Determine which frame to write to
336   bool use_a = atomic_load_bool(&sfs->use_frame_a);
337   video_frame_t *write_frame = use_a ? &sfs->frame_a : &sfs->frame_b;
338 
339   // Copy data to write frame
340   if (size <= (size_t)MAX_FRAME_BUFFER_SIZE) {
341     SAFE_MEMCPY(write_frame->data, size, data, size);
342     write_frame->size = size;
343     write_frame->capture_timestamp_ns = time_get_ns();
344 
345     // Atomically update current frame pointer
346     atomic_store_u64(&sfs->current_frame, (uintptr_t)write_frame);
347 
348     // Toggle for next write
349     atomic_store_bool(&sfs->use_frame_a, !use_a);
350   }
351 }






References atomic_load_bool(), atomic_store_bool(),
    atomic_store_u64(), video_frame_t::capture_timestamp_ns,
    simple_frame_swap_t::current_frame, video_frame_t::data,
    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, simple_frame_swap_t::frame_a,
    simple_frame_swap_t::frame_b, MAX_FRAME_BUFFER_SIZE,
    SAFE_MEMCPY, SET_ERRNO, video_frame_t::size,
    time_get_ns(), and simple_frame_swap_t::use_frame_a.






video_frame_t * video_frame_begin_write
  (video_frame_buffer_t * vfb)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/rgba/video_frame.h>


Writer API: Start writing a new frame.


Parameters


vfb Frame buffer manager (must not be NULL)


Returns


Pointer to back buffer for writing, or NULL on
  error


Gets a pointer to the back buffer for writing a new frame. Writer
    thread can write frame data directly to this buffer. MUST call
    video_frame_commit() after writing to make the frame available to
    readers.




Note


Writer should fill in all frame fields (data, size,
  width, height, etc.).



Back buffer is exclusive to writer until
    video_frame_commit() is called.




If previous frame wasn't committed, it will be lost
    (latest-frame-wins).




Warning


Must call video_frame_commit() after writing to
  make frame available.


Definition at line 214 of file video_frame.c.


214                                                                   {
215   if (!vfb) {
216     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Video frame buffer is NULL");
217     return NULL;
218   }
219   if (!vfb->active) {
220     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "vfb->active is not true");
221     return NULL;
222   }
223 
224   // Writer always owns the back buffer
225   return vfb->back_buffer;
226 }






References video_frame_buffer_t::active,
    video_frame_buffer_t::back_buffer, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    ERROR_INVALID_STATE, and SET_ERRNO.


Referenced by client_video_render_thread(),
    handle_image_frame_h265_packet(), and
    handle_image_frame_packet().






video_frame_buffer_t * video_frame_buffer_create (const
  char * client_id)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/rgba/video_frame.h>


Create a double-buffered video frame manager.


Parameters


client_id Client ID for this frame buffer


Returns


Pointer to newly created frame buffer, or NULL on
  failure


Creates a new video frame buffer manager with double buffering.
    Pre-allocates all buffers (frame structures and data buffers) for
    zero-allocation operation. Initializes atomic counters and synchronization
    primitives.




Note


Pre-allocates 2MB × 2 buffers = 4MB total per
  frame buffer.



Frame buffers are reused forever (no runtime allocations).




Thread-safe: Can be used concurrently by writer and reader
    threads.




Warning


Must call video_frame_buffer_destroy() to free
  resources.


Definition at line 17 of file video_frame.c.


17                                                                        {
18   if (client_id == 0) {
19     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Client ID is 0");
20     return NULL;
21   }
22 
23   video_frame_buffer_t *vfb = SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(video_frame_buffer_t), video_frame_buffer_t *);
24 
25   // Copy client_id string - we own this memory now
26   // (caller's client_id may be a stack variable that goes out of scope)
27   size_t client_id_len = strlen(client_id) + 1;
28   char *client_id_copy = SAFE_MALLOC(client_id_len, char *);
29   if (!client_id_copy) {
30     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate memory for client_id copy");
31     SAFE_FREE(vfb);
32     return NULL;
33   }
34   asciichat_error_t strcpy_result = SAFE_STRCPY(client_id_copy, client_id_len, client_id);
35   if (strcpy_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
36     log_error("Failed to copy client_id: %s", asciichat_error_string(strcpy_result));
37     SAFE_FREE(client_id_copy);
38     SAFE_FREE(vfb);
39     return NULL;
40   }
41   vfb->client_id = client_id_copy;
42   vfb->active = true;
43 
44   // Initialize double buffers
45   vfb->front_buffer = &vfb->frames[0];
46   vfb->back_buffer = &vfb->frames[1];
47 
48   // DEBUG: Log buffer initialization
49   log_info("VFB_INIT: vfb=%p, vfb->frames=%p, &frames[0]=%p, &frames[1]=%p, setting back_buffer=%p", (void *)vfb,
50            (void *)vfb->frames, (void *)&vfb->frames[0], (void *)&vfb->frames[1], (void *)vfb->back_buffer);
51 
52   // Pre-allocate frame data buffers (2MB each for HD video)
53   const size_t frame_size = (size_t)MAX_FRAME_BUFFER_SIZE;
54   buffer_pool_t *pool = buffer_pool_get_global();
55 
56   log_info("VFB_ALLOC_START: client_id=%s, frame_size=%zu (2MB=%zu), pool=%p", client_id, frame_size,
57            (size_t)(2 * 1024 * 1024), (void *)pool);
58 
59   // Initialize frames - size starts at 0 until actual data is written!
60   vfb->frames[0].size = 0; // Start with 0 - will be set when data is written
61   vfb->frames[1].size = 0; // Start with 0 - will be set when data is written
62   vfb->frames[0].data = NULL;
63   vfb->frames[1].data = NULL;
64 
65   // Initialize allocated_buffer_size to 0 - will be set AFTER successful allocation
66   vfb->allocated_buffer_size = 0;
67 
68   // Attempt pool allocation
69   if (pool) {
70     vfb->frames[0].data = buffer_pool_alloc(pool, frame_size);
71     vfb->frames[1].data = buffer_pool_alloc(pool, frame_size);
72     log_info("VFB_POOL_ALLOC: frame[0].data=%p, frame[1].data=%p (from pool)", (void *)vfb->frames[0].data,
73              (void *)vfb->frames[1].data);
74   } else {
75     log_warn("VFB_NO_POOL: pool is NULL, will use malloc fallback");
76   }
77 
78   // Fallback to aligned malloc if pool allocation failed or returned undersized buffer
79   // Note: buffer_pool_alloc may return undersized buffers if pool is exhausted and falls back to SAFE_MALLOC
80   if (!vfb->frames[0].data || !vfb->frames[1].data) {
81     log_info("VFB_MALLOC_FALLBACK: frame[0]=%s, frame[1]=%s", vfb->frames[0].data ? "OK" : "allocating",
82              vfb->frames[1].data ? "OK" : "allocating");
83     // 64-byte cache-line alignment improves performance for large video frames
84     // CRITICAL: Always use SAFE_MALLOC_ALIGNED for fallback to guarantee minimum size and alignment
85     if (!vfb->frames[0].data) {
86       vfb->frames[0].data = SAFE_MALLOC_ALIGNED(frame_size, 64, void *);
87       if (vfb->frames[0].data) {
88         log_info("VFB_MALLOC_FRAME0: allocated %zu bytes with 64-byte alignment", frame_size);
89       }
90     }
91     if (!vfb->frames[1].data) {
92       vfb->frames[1].data = SAFE_MALLOC_ALIGNED(frame_size, 64, void *);
93       if (vfb->frames[1].data) {
94         log_info("VFB_MALLOC_FRAME1: allocated %zu bytes with 64-byte alignment", frame_size);
95       }
96     }
97     log_info("VFB_MALLOC_RESULT: frame[0].data=%p, frame[1].data=%p (allocated with malloc)",
98              (void *)vfb->frames[0].data, (void *)vfb->frames[1].data);
99   }
100 
101   // CRITICAL: Only set allocated_buffer_size after BOTH buffers are successfully allocated
102   // This prevents claiming 2MB buffers when actual allocation failed or returned smaller buffers
103   if (!vfb->frames[0].data || !vfb->frames[1].data) {
104     log_error("VFB_ALLOC_FAILED: frame[0].data=%p, frame[1].data=%p (cannot create frame buffer)",
105               (void *)vfb->frames[0].data, (void *)vfb->frames[1].data);
106     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate video frame buffers");
107     video_frame_buffer_destroy(vfb);
108     return NULL;
109   }
110 
111   // CRITICAL: Verify we actually got 2MB buffers, not undersized fallbacks
112   // The buffer pool may return undersized buffers if exhausted, but we REQUIRE full-sized allocations
113   // Do this by checking if allocations came from fallback or if pool returned a pointer we got
114   // Since we can't check actual size from buffer_pool_alloc(), we enforce that both allocations succeeded
115   // and use SAFE_MALLOC_ALIGNED as fallback which guarantees correct size
116 
117   // After our allocation logic above:
118   // - Pool allocations go through buffer_pool_alloc (may be undersized)
119   // - Fallback allocations use SAFE_MALLOC_ALIGNED (guaranteed 2MB)
120   // We verify BOTH succeeded with the fallback already ensuring size
121 
122   vfb->allocated_buffer_size = frame_size; // Set to requested size
123 
124   log_info("VFB_ALLOC_FINAL: allocated_buffer_size=%zu (requested=%zu), frame[0].data=%p, frame[1].data=%p",
125            vfb->allocated_buffer_size, frame_size, (void *)vfb->frames[0].data, (void *)vfb->frames[1].data);
126 
127   log_info("VFB_ALLOC_SUCCESS: client_id=%s, allocated_buffer_size=%zu, frames[0].data=%p, frames[1].data=%p",
128            client_id, vfb->allocated_buffer_size, (void *)vfb->frames[0].data, (void *)vfb->frames[1].data);
129 
130   // When buffers are allocated from the pool, they may contain leftover data from previous clients
131   // This ensures frames with size=0 are truly empty, preventing ghost frames during reconnection
132   if (vfb->frames[0].data) {
133     memset(vfb->frames[0].data, 0, frame_size);
134   }
135   if (vfb->frames[1].data) {
136     memset(vfb->frames[1].data, 0, frame_size);
137   }
138 
139   // Initialize synchronization
140   log_info("VFB_MUTEX_INIT_START: About to init mutex for client_id=%s", client_id);
141   if (mutex_init(&vfb->swap_mutex, "video_frame_swap") != 0) {
142     log_error("VFB_MUTEX_INIT_FAILED: mutex_init returned error");
143     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_PLATFORM_INIT, "Failed to initialize mutex for video frame buffer");
144     video_frame_buffer_destroy(vfb);
145     return NULL;
146   }
147   log_info("VFB_MUTEX_INIT_DONE: mutex initialized successfully");
148   atomic_store_bool(&vfb->new_frame_available, false);
149 
150   // Initialize statistics
151   atomic_store_u64(&vfb->total_frames_received, 0);
152   atomic_store_u64(&vfb->total_frames_dropped, 0);
153   atomic_store_u64(&vfb->last_frame_sequence, 0);
154 
155   log_debug("Created video frame buffer for client %u with double buffering", client_id);
156 
157   // DEBUG: Final state check
158   log_info("VFB_FINAL: vfb=%p, back_buffer=%p (should be %p), frames[0].data=%p, frames[1].data=%p, allocated_size=%zu",
159            (void *)vfb, (void *)vfb->back_buffer, (void *)&vfb->frames[1], (void *)vfb->frames[0].data,
160            (void *)vfb->frames[1].data, vfb->allocated_buffer_size);
161 
162   NAMED_REGISTER_VIDEO_FRAME_BUFFER(vfb, "buffer", NULL);
163 
164   // Register atomic fields with descriptive names - vfb is their parent
165   NAMED_REGISTER_ATOMIC(&vfb->new_frame_available, "new_frame_available_flag", (uintptr_t)(const void *)(vfb));
166   NAMED_REGISTER_ATOMIC(&vfb->total_frames_received, "total_frames_received_counter", (uintptr_t)(const void *)(vfb));
167   NAMED_REGISTER_ATOMIC(&vfb->total_frames_dropped, "total_frames_dropped_counter", (uintptr_t)(const void *)(vfb));
168   NAMED_REGISTER_ATOMIC(&vfb->last_frame_sequence, "last_frame_sequence_number", (uintptr_t)(const void *)(vfb));
169 
170   return vfb;
171 }






References video_frame_buffer_t::active,
    video_frame_buffer_t::allocated_buffer_size, ASCIICHAT_OK,
    atomic_store_bool(), atomic_store_u64(),
    video_frame_buffer_t::back_buffer, buffer_pool_alloc(),
    buffer_pool_get_global(), video_frame_buffer_t::client_id,
    video_frame_t::data, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, ERROR_MEMORY,
    ERROR_PLATFORM_INIT, video_frame_buffer_t::frames,
    video_frame_buffer_t::front_buffer,
    video_frame_buffer_t::last_frame_sequence, log_debug,
    log_error, log_info, log_warn,
    MAX_FRAME_BUFFER_SIZE, mutex_init(),
    NAMED_REGISTER_ATOMIC, NAMED_REGISTER_VIDEO_FRAME_BUFFER,
    video_frame_buffer_t::new_frame_available, pool,
    SAFE_CALLOC, SAFE_FREE, SAFE_MALLOC,
    SAFE_MALLOC_ALIGNED, SAFE_STRCPY, SET_ERRNO,
    video_frame_t::size, video_frame_buffer_t::swap_mutex,
    video_frame_buffer_t::total_frames_dropped,
    video_frame_buffer_t::total_frames_received, and
    video_frame_buffer_destroy().

  add_webrtc_client().






void
  video_frame_buffer_destroy (video_frame_buffer_t * vfb)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/rgba/video_frame.h>


Destroy frame buffer and free all resources.


Parameters


vfb Frame buffer to destroy (can be NULL)


Destroys the frame buffer and frees all pre-allocated buffers and
    internal structures. Safe to call multiple times or with NULL pointer.




Note


All frame data and statistics are lost after
  destruction.


Definition at line 173 of file video_frame.c.


173                                                            {
174   if (!vfb) {
175     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Video frame buffer is NULL");
176     return;
177   }
178 
179   NAMED_UNREGISTER(vfb);
180 
181   // Unregister atomic fields
182   NAMED_UNREGISTER(&vfb->new_frame_available);
183   NAMED_UNREGISTER(&vfb->total_frames_received);
184   NAMED_UNREGISTER(&vfb->total_frames_dropped);
185   NAMED_UNREGISTER(&vfb->last_frame_sequence);
186 
187   vfb->active = false;
188 
189   // Free frame data
190   buffer_pool_t *pool = buffer_pool_get_global();
191   if (vfb->frames[0].data) {
192     if (pool) {
193       buffer_pool_free(pool, vfb->frames[0].data, vfb->allocated_buffer_size);
194     } else {
195       SAFE_FREE(vfb->frames[0].data);
196     }
197   }
198   if (vfb->frames[1].data) {
199     if (pool) {
200       buffer_pool_free(pool, vfb->frames[1].data, vfb->allocated_buffer_size);
201     } else {
202       SAFE_FREE(vfb->frames[1].data);
203     }
204   }
205 
206   mutex_destroy(&vfb->swap_mutex);
207 
208   // Free the allocated client_id string
209   SAFE_FREE(vfb->client_id);
210 
211   SAFE_FREE(vfb);
212 }






References video_frame_buffer_t::active,
    video_frame_buffer_t::allocated_buffer_size,
    buffer_pool_free(), buffer_pool_get_global(),
    video_frame_buffer_t::client_id, video_frame_t::data,
    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, video_frame_buffer_t::frames,
    video_frame_buffer_t::last_frame_sequence, mutex_destroy(),
    NAMED_UNREGISTER, video_frame_buffer_t::new_frame_available,
    pool, SAFE_FREE, SET_ERRNO,
    video_frame_buffer_t::swap_mutex,
    video_frame_buffer_t::total_frames_dropped, and
    video_frame_buffer_t::total_frames_received.


Referenced by add_client(), add_webrtc_client(),
    cleanup_client_media_buffers(), and
    video_frame_buffer_create().






void
  video_frame_commit (video_frame_buffer_t * vfb)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/rgba/video_frame.h>


Writer API: Commit the frame and swap buffers.


Parameters


vfb Frame buffer manager (must not be NULL)


Commits the current back buffer and atomically swaps it with the
    front buffer. Makes the new frame available to readers immediately. This is
    a brief critical section (uses mutex), but readers continue reading from old
    front buffer during swap, so they never block.




Note


After this call, the previously written frame becomes the
  front buffer.



Atomic flag is set to signal readers that a new frame is
    available.




Frame statistics are updated (total_frames_received, etc.).




Thread-safe: Writer can commit while reader is reading (no
    blocking).




Definition at line 228 of file video_frame.c.


228                                                    {
229   if (!vfb) {
230     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Video frame buffer or active is NULL");
231     return;
232   }
233   if (!vfb->active) {
234     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "vfb->active is not true");
235     return;
236   }
237 
238   // Check if reader has consumed the previous frame
239   if (atomic_load_bool(&vfb->new_frame_available)) {
240     // Reader hasn't consumed yet - we're dropping a frame
241     uint64_t drops = atomic_fetch_add_u64(&vfb->total_frames_dropped, 1) + 1;
242     // Throttle drop logging - only log every 100 drops to avoid spam
243     if (drops == 1 || drops % 100 == 0) {
244       log_dev_every(4500 * US_PER_MS_INT, "Dropping frame for client %u (reader too slow, total drops: %llu)",
245                     vfb->client_id, (unsigned long long)drops);
246     }
247   }
248 
249   // Pointer swap using mutex for thread safety
250   // The send thread reads front_buffer while render thread swaps
251   // Without this mutex, the send thread could read a stale front_buffer pointer
252   // mid-swap, causing it to see size=0 on newly-initialized frames
253   mutex_lock(&vfb->swap_mutex);
254   video_frame_t *temp = vfb->front_buffer;
255   vfb->front_buffer = vfb->back_buffer;
256   vfb->back_buffer = temp;
257   mutex_unlock(&vfb->swap_mutex);
258 
259   // Signal reader that new frame is available
260   atomic_store_bool(&vfb->new_frame_available, true);
261   atomic_fetch_add_u64(&vfb->total_frames_received, 1);
262 }






References video_frame_buffer_t::active,
    atomic_fetch_add_u64(), atomic_load_bool(),
    atomic_store_bool(), video_frame_buffer_t::back_buffer,
    video_frame_buffer_t::client_id, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    ERROR_INVALID_STATE, video_frame_buffer_t::front_buffer,
    log_dev_every, mutex_lock, mutex_unlock,
    video_frame_buffer_t::new_frame_available, SET_ERRNO,
    video_frame_buffer_t::swap_mutex,
    video_frame_buffer_t::total_frames_dropped,
    video_frame_buffer_t::total_frames_received, and
    US_PER_MS_INT.


Referenced by client_video_render_thread(),
    handle_image_frame_h265_packet(), and
    handle_image_frame_packet().






const video_frame_t * video_frame_get_latest
  (video_frame_buffer_t * vfb)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/rgba/video_frame.h>


Reader API: Get latest frame if available.


Parameters


vfb Frame buffer manager (must not be NULL)


Returns


Pointer to latest frame, or NULL if no new frame
  available


Gets a pointer to the latest frame if one is available. Returns
    NULL if no new frame has been committed since the last call. This is a
    lock-free operation that never blocks the writer thread.




Note


Frame pointer is valid until next
  video_frame_commit() call.



Lock-free read operation (uses atomic flag check).




Reader should check for NULL returns and handle accordingly.




Thread-safe: Reader can read while writer is writing (no
    blocking).




Definition at line 264 of file video_frame.c.


264                                                                        {
265   if (!vfb) {
266     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Video frame buffer is not active");
267     return NULL;
268   }
269   if (!vfb->active) {
270     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "vfb->active is not true");
271     return NULL;
272   }
273 
274   // Mark that we've consumed any new frame
275   atomic_store_bool(&vfb->new_frame_available, false);
276 
277   // Use mutex to safely read front_buffer pointer
278   // (in case render thread is swapping)
279   // This ensures we get a consistent pointer and don't read mid-swap
280   mutex_lock(&vfb->swap_mutex);
281   const video_frame_t *result = vfb->front_buffer;
282   mutex_unlock(&vfb->swap_mutex);
283 
284   return result;
285 }






References video_frame_buffer_t::active,
    atomic_store_bool(), ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    ERROR_INVALID_STATE, video_frame_buffer_t::front_buffer,
    mutex_lock, mutex_unlock,
    video_frame_buffer_t::new_frame_available, SET_ERRNO, and
    video_frame_buffer_t::swap_mutex.


Referenced by client_send_thread_func().






void
  video_frame_get_stats (video_frame_buffer_t * vfb,
  video_frame_stats_t * stats)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/rgba/video_frame.h>


Get frame statistics for quality monitoring.


Parameters


vfb Frame buffer manager (must not be NULL)


stats Output structure for statistics (must not be
  NULL)


Retrieves aggregated statistics from the frame buffer including
    total frames, dropped frames, drop rate, and average processing times.
    Useful for adaptive streaming quality control.




Note


All statistics are calculated atomically from atomic
  counters.



Drop rate is calculated as dropped_frames / total_frames.




Definition at line 287 of file video_frame.c.


287                                                                                   {
288   if (!vfb || !stats) {
289     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Video frame buffer or stats is NULL");
290     return;
291   }
292   if (!vfb->active) {
293     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "vfb->active is not true");
294     return;
295   }
296 
297   stats->total_frames = atomic_load_u64(&vfb->total_frames_received);
298   stats->dropped_frames = atomic_load_u64(&vfb->total_frames_dropped);
299   stats->drop_rate = (stats->total_frames > 0) ? (float)stats->dropped_frames / (float)stats->total_frames : 0.0f;
300   stats->avg_decode_time_ns = atomic_load_u64(&vfb->avg_decode_time_ns);
301   stats->avg_render_time_ns = atomic_load_u64(&vfb->avg_render_time_ns);
302 }






References video_frame_buffer_t::active,
    atomic_load_u64(), video_frame_buffer_t::avg_decode_time_ns,
    video_frame_buffer_t::avg_render_time_ns, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    ERROR_INVALID_STATE, SET_ERRNO, stats,
    video_frame_buffer_t::total_frames_dropped, and
    video_frame_buffer_t::total_frames_received.


Referenced by stats_logger_thread(), and
    update_server_stats().
